title: celtis palauensis
intro: Celtis palauensis, also known as Palauan hackberry or Palau hackberry, is a plant species native to the island of Palau in the Pacific Ocean. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities. The health benefits of Celtis palauensis include its ability to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. The plant can be prepared into various herbal remedies, such as teas, infusions, and extracts, which are often consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
